questions.question: Define Display List and explain its primary purpose in computer graphics.
keyPoints: Display list is a data structure storing graphics commands for later execution
Primary purpose is to improve rendering performance by reducing parsing overhead
Also known as display file or display program
Allows repeated execution of graphics commands without re-parsing

questions.question: Differentiate between Immediate Mode and Retained Mode rendering.
keyPoints: Immediate mode sends commands directly to pipeline for execution
Retained mode stores commands in display lists for later use
Immediate mode requires resending all commands for redraw
Retained mode retains scene in memory for efficient re-rendering

questions.question: List and explain three types of Display Lists.
keyPoints: Linear/Simple display list - stores commands sequentially in order
Hierarchical display list - organizes commands in tree structure with parent-child relationships
Indexed display list - uses indices for efficient storage and retrieval
Each type has different advantages for different graphics scenarios

questions.question: What is a Hierarchical Display List? Explain its advantages.
keyPoints: Hierarchical display list organizes graphics in tree structure
Supports parent-child relationships for transformations
Allows compound object modeling from simpler sub-objects
Transformations applied to parent automatically affect children
Promotes modular design of complex graphics objects

questions.question: Explain the concept of open and closed Display Lists.
keyPoints: Open display lists allow modifications after creation
New commands can be added or existing ones edited in open lists
Closed display lists are fixed after creation
Closed lists cannot be modified, optimized for static objects
Open lists provide flexibility but may have slightly lower performance

questions.question: What are the advantages of using Display Lists in graphics applications?
keyPoints: Improved performance through reduced parsing overhead
Memory efficiency by storing commands once and executing multiple times
Support for complex object modeling through hierarchical structures
Encapsulation promotes object-oriented graphics design
Efficient for applications requiring repeated rendering of same objects like CAD

questions.question: Explain the role of a Display List Interpreter in graphics rendering.
keyPoints: Reads commands stored in the display list
Translates commands into actual graphics output
Acts as a virtual graphics machine executing stored programs
Coordinates between display list storage and graphics hardware
Enables the separation of command specification from execution
